Description: Lower support labor and/or client provides more scope (facilities, tools). Typical for high-skill/niche service types (e.g. software engineering, architecture) with lighter TL/QA intensity.
Typical managed-service delivery: standard support ratios (TL, QA, etc.) and typical vendor vs client scope. Default for most service types.
Heavy support labor and/or vendor provides full scope (facilities, equipment, workflow tools). Typical for L1 customer service, high volume, many juniors, high QA intensity.
